files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 9024b84..7faf3f3 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -3,26 +3,26 @@
 ## Critical mechanics / lessons
 - Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent/replaced. Do not call a separate rationale logger.
-- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'`. Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond immediately (docs conflict 3 vs 7 days, so same-week is mandatory).
-- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshots/replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly (e.g. group promo 0). `set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={})` clears targeted ops.
-- Social posts with concrete quota/reliability fixes have worked very well; generic posts underperform. Protect perceived quality: in-app ads strength remains 0.
+- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'`. Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond same week (no open threads at day42).
+- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshots/replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly. `set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={})` clears targeted ops.
+- In-app ads strength remains 0; quality/reputation is more valuable than small ad revenue. Concrete social replies with exact quota/reliability fixes get strong positive reactions.
 
-## State at Day 35 before Week 5 actions
-- Cash $345,312; 90 individual subs, 0 enterprise seats, 5 open issues (all opened day35, global ops has been resolving in ~1 day). No overload/outage; tier0 capacity still ample (peak 4,103/day vs 50k/day capacity).
-- Active subs/MRR: S1 11 / $183, S2 50 / $2,254, S3 29 / $1,101. No churn yet. Current MRR list basis about $3.5k; many old cohorts are locked at low prices.
-- Week day29-35 economics under old config: costs dev $4.2k, ads $3.85k, ops $3.5k, capacity $595, lead cost $188, compute $37; subscription cash $1,829; net burn $10.54k.
-- Acquisition day29-35: S2 content $2,450 -> 285 leads -> 25 subs (2 B, strong CAC/payback); S3 content $700 -> 37 leads -> 9 subs; S3 social $700 -> 47 leads -> 5 subs. S3 first-month $20 lead promo revived conversion (initial payments mostly $29, recurring list $49).
-- Quality after R&D t1 completion: global bonus 0.0643; S2 bonus 0.3433 (A q .456), S3 bonus 0.2532 (A q .388). R&D t1_1 completed day30 (+0.0289). No new R&D; tier2 costs $333k, too risky now.
-- Social day35 had another S2/S3 quota/hard-stop complaint cluster despite positive day29 reactions. Users value quality but hate mid-run stops.
+## State at Day 42 before Week 6 actions
+- Cash $336,245; 210 individual subs, 0 enterprise seats; 17 open issues all opened day42 (ops resolved 79 issues last week at age 1). No outage/overload; tier0 peak 18,852/day vs 50k capacity.
+- Active subs/MRR: S1 10/$168; S2 135/$6,739 (129 A, 6 B); S3 65/$3,335 (60 A, 4 B, 1 C). Total recurring list/effective MRR about $10.24k. Only churn so far: 1 old S1.
+- Week day36-42 economics: subscription cash $5,292 vs costs ads $5,600, dev $4,200, ops $3,500, capacity $595, compute $193, lead cost $271 => net burn ~$9.1k.
+- Acquisition day36-42: 542 leads -> 121 subs. S2 content $3,500 -> 417 leads -> 83 subs (CAC ~$43/sub before first revenue). S3 content $1,400 -> 71 leads -> 19 subs; S3 social $700 -> 45 leads -> 17 subs (best S3 channel). Network added a few.
+- Quality before tier upgrade: global bonus 0.0683; S2 group bonus 0.3510; S3 0.2608. With old A2/B3/C4 delivered quality was S2 A .4645, S3 A .3968. Social day42 again complained about paid caps/usage ceilings despite positive day36 post.
 
-## Week 5 actions for Days 36-42
-- Kept prices A/B/C = $49/$129/$199. Model tiers A2/B3/C4 (C now higher quality differentiator). Raised quotas substantially to A=800, B=2500, C=6000 to stop paid workflow hard-stops while preserving paid upgrade path.
-- Spend: ops $500/d; global dev $100/d; targeted dev S2 $250/d + S3 $250/d; no targeted ops; capacity tier0 for now.
-- Ads scaled moderately: content_marketing S2 $500/d, content_marketing S3 $200/d, social_media S3 $100/d (total $800/d). No enterprise paid ads until quality/pipeline economics improve; prior D_E09 LinkedIn/content produced zero leads.
-- Promos: ongoing promo 0. Lead promos S2=$10 first month, S3=$20 first month, all others 0. Hypothesis: S2 promo will improve conversion like S3 without hurting recurring unit economics.
-- Posted day35 reply to post 61 (agent_post_id 6): announced Starter/Pro/Team 800/2500/6000, 80/95% pre-flight warnings, resumable checkpoints, safe pause instead of failing. Check views/reactions next week.
+## Week 6 actions for Days 43-49
+- Kept prices A/B/C = $49/$129/$199; upgraded model tiers to A3/B4/C5 because compute cost is small vs ARPU and quality should lift conversion/retention.
+- Raised quotas to A=1,200/day, B=6,000/day, C=20,000/day to address professional usage ceiling complaints while preserving a clear Pro/Team headroom path.
+- Scaled capacity to tier1 (200k/day) to protect against fast growth/usage spikes. Ops raised to $750/d. Global dev $100/d; targeted dev S2 $250/d + S3 $350/d; targeted ops cleared.
+- Ads scaled aggressively but still on proven channels: content_marketing S2 $1,200/d, content_marketing S3 $300/d, social_media S3 $500/d (total $2,000/d). No enterprise/new-market spend yet.
+- Promos: no ongoing promo. Lead promos S2=$15 first month, S3=$20 first month, all others 0. Hypothesis: S2 $15 + A3 quality improves conversion beyond last week’s 20% without hurting renewal economics.
+- Posted day42 reply to post 72 (agent_post_id 7): old caps made pro work feel rationed; announced 1,200/6,000/20,000 quotas, pre-run usage meter/80-95% alerts, checkpoints, Pro stays $129.
 
 ## Strategy / watch next week
-- Day42 targets: cash around $334k; 55-80 new subs would validate the ad+promo scale. S2 content target >=40 subs/week at $500/d with $10 promo; S3 content/social target >=20 subs/week combined.
-- Watch quota complaints and compute/capacity. If daily usage approaches 25k+ or p95/errors rise, consider tier1 capacity; if compute per sub jumps materially, tighten A quota or push B/C upgrades later.
-- Watch renewals/churn as old cohorts bill. If no churn and conversion holds, continue scaling profitable S2/S3 content gradually (1.5x-2x) before considering R&D. If S3 social CAC remains worse than content, shift more S3 budget from social to content.
-- Avoid market research / new R&D / enterprise paid ads until MRR and cash trend improve. Respond to any enterprise threads immediately.
+- Day49 targets: cash roughly $322k-$328k; 1,100-1,500 leads and 250-450 new subs would validate scaling. If actual CAC/first-payment remains near cash-neutral, continue scaling S2 content and S3 social/content gradually.
+- Watch quota complaint volume and actual usage. If daily usage approaches 140k+ or p95/errors rise, consider tier2. If issues age beyond 1 day, raise ops or add targeted ops by S2/S3/Plan A.
+- Watch plan mix: B/C should become more attractive after B4/C5 and larger quotas. If all users still choose A and cap complaints persist, consider lowering B to $99 or further increasing B/C quotas rather than raising A much more.
+- Avoid market research, new R&D, and enterprise paid ads until MRR/cash trend strengthens. Consider another R&D only after cash comfortably exceeds ~$500k or quality drift threatens retention.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Week 6 actions (Day 42): scale profitable S2/S3 acquisition while fixing quota/headroom complaints.
print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)

print('pricing', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=49, B=129, C=199))
# Increase model quality across tiers; compute cost is still tiny vs subscription ARPU.
print('tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=4, C=5))
# Larger professional-grade headroom with a clear paid upgrade path.
print('quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1200, B=6000, C=20000))

# Capacity tier 1 protects the now-rapidly growing user base from quota/latency surprises.
print('capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=1))

# Keep reliability spending high as issues scale; continue quality compounding for S2/S3.
print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=750, development=100))
print('targeted_dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 250, "S3": 350}))
print('targeted_ops_clear', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={}))

# Scale known profitable channels. Social on S3 outperformed S3 content; S2 content remains best volume.
ad_spend = {
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 1200, "S3": 300},
    "social_media": {"S3": 500},
    "search_ads": {},
    "linkedin": {},
    "referral_program": {},
}
print('ads', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=ad_spend))

# First-month discounts are targeted to high-LTV S2/S3 only; no ongoing discounting.
known = {"S1": 0.0, "S2": 15.0, "S3": 20.0, "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0}
print('lead_promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=known, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))
print('ongoing_promo', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={}))
print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# Respond publicly to the new quota complaint cluster with the exact changed limits.
content = "You're right—the old caps made daily pro work feel like rationing. Today: Starter 1,200/day, Pro 6,000, Team 20,000. Pre-run usage meter + 80/95% alerts now stop unsafe starts; checkpoints resume over-cap jobs. Pro stays $129."
print('post', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=72))

import json, math
import novamind_api as nm

print('current_day', nm.vars.current_day)
# Basic ledger weekly/day/category
queries = {
'cash_calc': "SELECT SUM(amount) as cash FROM ledger",
'ledger_by_cat_7': "SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount, COUNT(*) n FROM ledger WHERE day>35 AND day<=42 GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount DESC",
'ledger_by_day_14': "SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount FROM ledger WHERE day>28 GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category",
'subs_by_group_plan': """
 SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr_eff, SUM(s.listed_price*s.seat_count) mrr_list,
        AVG(s.effective_price) avg_price, MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE s.status='subscribed'
 GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan""",
'subs_by_start_week': """
 SELECT (s.start_day/7)*7 as wk_start, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) mrr_eff
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE s.status='subscribed'
 GROUP BY wk_start,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY wk_start,c.group_id,s.plan""",
'churned_lost_last14': """
 SELECT s.status, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.end_day) max_end
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE (s.status!='subscribed') AND (COALESCE(s.end_day,s.start_day)>28)
 GROUP BY s.status,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY s.status,c.group_id,s.plan""",
'ad_leads_last14': """
 SELECT day, channel_id, group_id, SUM(leads_generated) leads, ROUND(SUM(spend),2) spend,
        CASE WHEN SUM(leads_generated)>0 THEN ROUND(SUM(spend)*1.0/SUM(leads_generated),2) ELSE NULL END cpl
 FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>28 GROUP BY day,channel_id,group_id ORDER BY day,channel_id,group_id""",
'ad_summary_last7': """
 SELECT channel_id, group_id, SUM(leads_generated) leads, ROUND(SUM(spend),2) spend,
        CASE WHEN SUM(leads_generated)>0 THEN ROUND(SUM(spend)*1.0/SUM(leads_generated),2) ELSE NULL END cpl
 FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>35 AND day<=42 GROUP BY channel_id, group_id ORDER BY group_id, channel_id""",
'customers_created_last7_by_source': """
 SELECT c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,COUNT(*) customers,
        SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) subscribed,
        ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN s.effective_price END),2) avg_eff
 FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
 WHERE c.created_day>35 AND c.created_day<=42
 GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source ORDER BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source""",
'issues_open': """
 SELECT i.group_id, i.status, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n, MIN(i.open_day) min_open, MAX(i.open_day) max_open
 FROM issues i WHERE i.status='open'
 GROUP BY i.group_id,i.status,i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id,i.days_open""",
'issues_resolved_last7': """
 SELECT group_id, resolved_day-open_day as age, COUNT(*) n
 FROM issues WHERE status='resolved' AND resolved_day>35 GROUP BY group_id, age ORDER BY group_id, age""",
'service_last14': "SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day>28 ORDER BY day",
'config_overrides': "SELECT * FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 20",
'config_history_last': "SELECT * FROM config_history ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 10",
'ent_open': "SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 ORDER BY day, customer_id, message_id LIMIT 100",
'research_projects': "SELECT * FROM research_projects ORDER BY started_day DESC, project_id",
'groups_info': "SELECT * FROM group_info_levels ORDER BY group_id",
'agent_posts': "SELECT * FROM agent_social_media_posts ORDER BY day DESC, agent_post_id DESC LIMIT 10",
'recent_notifications': "SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE day>=28 ORDER BY day DESC, notification_id DESC LIMIT 30",
'lead_status_counts_last7': """
 SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, COUNT(*) n
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE s.start_day>35 AND s.start_day<=42
 GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status ORDER BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,s.status""",
# approximate active planned billing next week
'billing_next7': """
 SELECT ((30 + s.billing_day_mod30 - (42 % 30)) % 30) as days_until_bill, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) revenue
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND ((30 + s.billing_day_mod30 - (42 % 30)) % 30) BETWEEN 1 AND 7
 GROUP BY days_until_bill,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY days_until_bill,c.group_id,s.plan""",
'billing_next30_by_day': """
 SELECT ((30 + s.billing_day_mod30 - (42 % 30)) % 30) as days_until_bill, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) revenue
 FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id
 WHERE s.status='subscribed'
 GROUP BY days_until_bill ORDER BY days_until_bill""",
'usage_by_group_plan_last7': """
 SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT du.customer_id) users, SUM(du.usage_units) usage,
        ROUND(AVG(du.usage_units),1) avg_daily_usage, ROUND(MAX(du.usage_units),1) max_daily_usage
 FROM daily_usage du JOIN customers c ON du.customer_id=c.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON du.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
 WHERE du.day>35 AND du.day<=42
 GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan""",
'usage_by_day': "SELECT day, SUM(usage_units) usage, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) users FROM daily_usage WHERE day>35 AND day<=42 GROUP BY day ORDER BY day",
'satisfaction_proxy_bills': """SELECT c.group_id,s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.listed_price),2) list, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) eff, ROUND(AVG(42-s.start_day),1) avg_age FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id WHERE s.status='subscribed' GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan"""
}
for name,q in queries.items():
    print('\n###',name)
    try:
        r=nm.query(q)
        rows=r.get('rows', [])
        print(json.dumps(rows[:200], indent=2))
        if len(rows)>200: print('... rows', len(rows))
        if r.get('warning'): print('WARNING', r['warning'])
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR', type(e).__name__, e)

print('\n### cost_info')
try:
    print(json.dumps(nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), indent=2))
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e)

print('\n### research_list')
try:
    print(json.dumps(nm.research.list_research_projects(), indent=2))
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e)

print('\n### social_posts')
try:
    posts=nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=100)
    print(json.dumps(posts, indent=2))
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e)

print('\n### market_overview')
try:
    print(json.dumps(nm.market.get_market_overview(), indent=2)[:6000])
except Exception as e: print('ERR',e)
